title: Tiling randomly perturbed bipartite graphs
summary: A perfect $H$-tiling in a graph $G$ is a collection of vertex-disjoint copies of a graph $H$ in $G$ that covers all vertices of $G$. Motivated by papers of Bush and Zhao and of Balogh, Treglown, and Wagner, we determine the threshold for the existence of a perfect $K_{h,h}$-tiling of a randomly perturbed bipartite graph with linear minimum degree.
